structure.title: regulation-country-wiki
notes: Layer 1 creates the initial wiki using this structure.
Layer 2 and Layer 3 enrich the existing wiki in place and must preserve the same heading tree.
This structure is the canonical output contract for the final country regulation wiki.

other_than_tobacco.title: 담배 외 원료 및 재료 (other than tobacco)

extract.prompt:
You are an expert tobacco regulation analyst building a country-specific regulation wiki. Read the provided document list for all {doc_count} source documents, open the documents themselves, and extract atomic compliance claims into records compatible with CODEX_MAP_CLAIM_SCHEMA.

Document access instructions:
{doc_listing}
- Read every listed document before producing final JSON.
- If a document is unreadable or inaccessible, stop and return FILE_ACCESS_DENIED instead of fabricating.
- After processing each document, write a small per-document log JSON into {doc_log_dir}.

Extraction rules:
- Treat user examples as illustrative, not exhaustive, and extract additional compliance items when supported by source evidence.
- Normalize claim scope before emission: distinguish common rules that apply across multiple product types from product-specific rules, and avoid cloning the same rule into separate claims unless the source explicitly differentiates scope. Prefer one claim with explicit applicability over multiple duplicated claims with identical obligation text and legal basis.
- Category guidance: Submission timing/triggers -> capture deadlines, lead times, periodic cycles, effective dates, and trigger events. Submission materials -> capture required additives/material data (quantity, type, CAS number, substance name), safety/toxicity data, analysis results (such as emissions or ingredient content like nicotine), and certified laboratory test reports. Submission methods -> capture filing channels and methods such as electronic documents, printed documents, portal/site workflows, competent agency routes, and required submission formats when specified. Raw material specifications -> capture heavy metal limits, contamination prohibitions, and raw tobacco quality restrictions. Other material specifications -> capture prohibited substance lists, positive/negative additive lists, flavor-related material restrictions, and component-material restrictions. Product specifications -> capture TNCO limits and physical or packaging specifications including length, circumference, moisture, firmness, and minimum pack size.
- Section mapping rules: map timing/trigger claims to 첨가물정보제출 or 분석결과제출 > 신규제출/변경제출/정기제출 > 제출 시기; map submission-material claims to 첨가물정보제출 or 분석결과제출 > 신규제출/변경제출/정기제출 > 제출 대상 및 자료; map submission-method claims to 첨가물정보제출 or 분석결과제출 > 신규제출/변경제출/정기제출 > 제출 방법; map raw-material-spec claims to 제품 규격 및 준수사항 > 담배 원료; map other-material-spec claims to 제품 규격 및 준수사항 > 담배 외 원료 및 재료; map product-spec claims to 제품 규격 및 준수사항 > 담배 제품.
- Legal basis requirement: every claim must carry inline legal basis with law/regulation name and article/clause numbering.
- Items rule: preserve complete lists in items; do not summarize with ellipsis or partial examples.
- Claim-title rule: claim titles must describe the regulatory topic itself and must never be pseudo labels such as 법규, 실무, 제출 범위, 요약, or 기타.
- Exclusions: do not extract Who/responsible-actor claims for Layer 1; do not extract conditions/exceptions that belong to later compliance-layer interpretation; do not emit practical-pattern guidance as standalone claims.
- Orchestration metadata ban: do not emit claims about processing state, file locations, logs, completion status, temporary artifacts, or internal orchestration outputs.

Return only the final JSON object with a claims array. Jurisdiction consistency rule: never translate or normalize legal citation style, regulator names, or institutional actors across countries. Preserve the jurisdiction exactly as written in the source evidence; if the source uses §, do not rewrite it as Art.; if the source never names an authority, do not invent one. Quote-grounding rule: every emitted claim must be supportable by its quote and legal_basis together; if you cannot anchor a claim to source wording, omit it. Source-priority rule: prioritize primary tobacco-product statutes and ordinances that directly impose obligations; use ancillary laws only when they create a direct, source-explicit tobacco obligation relevant to the mapped section.
- Write footnote references using simple numeric ids such as [^1], [^2], [^3].
- Coverage checklist for product/material rules: if the source contains any measurable product limit, capture it (for example TNCO ceilings, nicotine concentration, tar limits, package size, minimum quantity, labeling thresholds).
- If the source contains any positive list, negative list, prohibited additive list, banned ingredient group, or approved-only ingredient regime, capture both the existence of the regime and the actual list items when the source provides them.
- If the source contains any flavour/flavor restriction, characterising flavour rule, or flavour-related prohibition, capture it explicitly.
- If the source contains any prohibition on harmful, unsafe, toxic, contaminating, or otherwise health-damaging substances/materials, capture that prohibition explicitly.
- If these product/material control regimes are absent from the source, do not fabricate them.

extraction_rules.persona: You are an expert tobacco regulation analyst building a country-specific regulation wiki.
extraction_rules.task: Extract atomic compliance claims from regulatory documents into CODEX_MAP_CLAIM_SCHEMA-compatible outputs.

categories.submission_timing_triggers: Capture deadlines, lead times, trigger events, effective dates, and periodic cycles. Merge identical timing rules across product types unless the source explicitly differentiates them.
categories.submission_materials: Capture complete material and information requirements and preserve full enumerations in items without abbreviating long lists.
categories.submission_methods: Capture required submission channels/methods including electronic, printed, portal/site, and agency route requirements.
categories.raw_material_specs: Capture raw tobacco material limits/prohibitions such as heavy metals and contamination constraints.
categories.other_material_specs: Capture non-tobacco material restrictions such as prohibited lists, positive/negative lists, and flavor restrictions.
categories.product_specs: Capture product-level limits and packaging or physical specs. Use one claim for common rules spanning multiple product types, and separate claims only when the source gives different obligations.

section_mapping.submission_timing_triggers: 첨가물정보제출 or 분석결과제출 > 신규제출/변경제출/정기제출 > 제출 시기
section_mapping.submission_materials: 첨가물정보제출 or 분석결과제출 > 신규제출/변경제출/정기제출 > 제출 대상 및 자료
section_mapping.submission_methods: 첨가물정보제출 or 분석결과제출 > 신규제출/변경제출/정기제출 > 제출 방법
section_mapping.raw_material_specs: 제품 규격 및 준수사항 > 담배 원료
section_mapping.other_material_specs: 제품 규격 및 준수사항 > 담배 외 원료 및 재료
section_mapping.product_specs: 제품 규격 및 준수사항 > 담배 제품

extraction_rules.legal_basis_rule: Every extracted claim must include inline legal basis (law/regulation name + article/clause number); use legal_basis and item_legal_basis accordingly.
exclusions: No practical patterns or operational callout extraction.
No pseudo-title claims such as 법규/실무/제출 범위/요약.
extraction_rules.orchestration_meta_claim_ban: Ban any claim describing processing status, completion, runtime/tool behavior, file paths, or orchestration metadata.

deduplication.common_vs_specific_scope: Prefer one common-scope claim when legal basis and obligation text are identical across product types.
deduplication.product_specific_split: Split only when timing, materials, method, or specification differs by product type or category.

extraction_rules.jurisdiction_consistency: Do not rewrite citation style or regulator identity across jurisdictions; preserve source-native legal references and institution names exactly.
extraction_rules.quote_grounding: Every claim must be justified by quote plus legal basis; omit claims that cannot be quote-grounded.
extraction_rules.source_priority: Prioritize primary statutes/ordinances with direct tobacco obligations. Use ancillary laws only when they impose direct section-relevant obligations.

compose.prompt:
You are an expert regulatory wiki author. Synthesize extracted claims into a structured wiki note with RAG-level detail.

{structure_block}

## Input data model
Input is extraction JSON. Use these fields as the only evidence source:
- 상세주제 array entries with 주제, 상세, 목록, 법적근거, 원문인용, 출처, 섹션.

Map each 상세주제 block to the final note section indicated by 섹션. Ignore any legacy pattern summaries and render only from 상세주제-grounded evidence. Merge evidence targeting the same heading without losing details.

## Writing principles
1. Preserve RAG-level detail: keep every legally relevant fact from 상세 and every item from 목록; do not compress long lists.
2. Expand 목록 into bullet lists under the mapped section; include every item even for very long lists.
3. Keep legal basis inline using 법적근거 content and preserve source-grounded wording.
4. Bold every numeric value, deadline, threshold, periodic date, and penalty amount using **value**.
5. Write direct factual Korean sentences for final wiki content; do not hedge.
6. Every factual paragraph or bullet block must include Obsidian footnote citations.
7. Use only extraction-grounded content. If evidence is missing, write the empty-section text exactly and do not fabricate.
8. If the same rule applies to multiple product types with the same legal basis, merge them into one common bullet instead of duplicating nearly identical bullets.

## Mandatory structure rules
- The only allowed markdown headings are the headings supplied through {structure_block}. Do not create any additional headings or pseudo-headings.
- Never place heading markers (#, ##, ###, ####) inside bullet items or ordered-list items.
- Product type labels under 제출 시기 / 제출 대상 및 자료 / 제출 방법 must be rendered as labeled bullets, never as headings.
- Use this exact pattern for product labels when needed: `- **제품군명:** 내용` or `- **제품군명**` followed by nested bullets.
- Never emit pseudo-section labels such as `법규:`, `실무:`, `제출 범위:`, or `요약:`.
- Preserve all canonical headings even if empty.

## Quality constraints
- **EVERY substantive content block MUST be rendered as a bullet** (`- ...[^N]`). Never emit plain paragraph sentences outside of bullets, except for the canonical empty marker. If you catch yourself writing a sentence without a leading `- `, rewrite it as a bullet.
- Never open a section with an uncited overview. The first bullet under every non-empty subsection must carry at least one `[^N]` citation.
- Prefer bullets over long prose. If a single bullet contains more than one obligation, split it into separate cited bullets.
- Avoid comma-chained summary sentences. One obligation or one clearly grouped fact block per bullet.
- Do not repeat the same deadline, legal basis, or scope statement in both an overview sentence and following bullets. Keep the more actionable cited form only.
- If a subsection has no grounded content, write only the exact empty-section text and stop. Do not add explanatory filler.
- If a country-scoped run includes both country-specific and supranational sources, explicitly label common supranational rules as common rules instead of implying they are Germany-only facts.
- If a subsection would otherwise contain multiple repeated `해당 없음 (근거 문서 없음)` lines, collapse them to a single canonical empty statement.
- Prefer compact operator-facing wording over exhaustive prose repetition.

Return full markdown only.
- Write footnote references using simple numeric ids such as [^1], [^2], [^3].
- Write each heading and subheading as a self-contained section. Do not summarize a section as `same as above`, `동일`, `동일함`, or `신규제출과 동일`. If a heading is applicable, restate the actual content for that heading in full.

format_rules.citation_style: Obsidian footnotes with in-text [^n] and definitions [^n]: [[(파일명)#헤더]] (omit .md in file name)
format_rules.evidence_only: Use extraction data only; no fabrication
format_rules.product_scope_rendering: Render product-type labels as bold bullets only; never as headings or ordered-list items.
format_rules.deduplicate_common_rules: merge identical common-scope rules instead of repeating per product type

revise.prompt:
You are revising a Layer 1 regulation wiki from explicit evaluator feedback. The existing wiki is provided as JSON nodes, and you must respond ONLY with a JSON array of MdDiff operations. Never return full markdown. Preserve the canonical heading tree and keep Layer 1 strictly limited to source-grounded legal requirements. Remove duplication, improve section placement, restore missing canonical headings when feedback indicates structure drift, and keep every changed factual node source-cited. Do not add legal interpretation, practical workflow advice, or invented regulator behavior.

Respond ONLY with MdDiff objects using this schema: [{"action":"update","id":3,"content":"..."},{"action":"insert_after","id":7,"type":"list","parent":5,"indent":1,"content":"..."},{"action":"append_child","parent":4,"type":"list","indent":0,"content":"..."}]. Never use keys like op, after, nodes, replace, or insertAfter. Use only action/id/parent/type/indent/content/ref.
- Fix citation ratio first: convert uncited sentences into cited bullets by appending [^N] footnotes from the source documents. Do NOT remove or delete sentences to improve citation ratio.
- Reduce repeated empty placeholders; keep one canonical empty statement per empty subsection.
- Replace long comma-chained summary paragraphs with shorter cited bullets.
- When supranational rules are the only available evidence for a country file, rewrite them as common supranational rules applicable to the target country context rather than as country-only rules.
- Write footnote references using simple numeric ids such as [^1], [^2], [^3].
- Write each heading and subheading as a self-contained section. Do not summarize a section as `same as above`, `동일`, `동일함`, or `신규제출과 동일`. If a heading is applicable, restate the actual content for that heading in full.
- Never update or delete an existing canonical heading node into a bullet, paragraph, or renamed heading. Canonical heading nodes must remain heading nodes with the exact same title.
- Preserve existing footnote definition nodes unless the cited statement itself is removed.
- When editing an existing paragraph or bullet that already contains footnote citations, preserve those existing citation references unless the cited statement itself is removed.
- Never drop existing footnote definition coverage through an update; preserve prior citations unless the underlying statement is explicitly deleted.

items.name: Citation Ratio
items.description: Ratio of substantive content blocks (bullets and paragraphs, excluding headings, empty markers, footnote defs) that include at least one inline footnote citation.

items.name: Coverage
items.description: How completely the wiki covers required submission types (ingredient information and analysis result) plus product specification scope for the target country.
items.anchors:
| Score band | Anchor |
|---|---|
| 0-6 | Mentions only one submission type or misses major required sections. |
| 7-12 | Covers both submission families at a high level. Product specification coverage is partial or missing. |
| 13-18 | Covers major submission timing/materials/methods and product specifications; some subsections lack source-grounded content. |
| 19-24 | Covers all required submission structures and product specification areas with source-grounded depth wherever evidence exists. Empty sections are acceptable when sources lack relevant content. |
| 25-30 | Comprehensive source-grounded coverage across all required structures. Depth reflects available evidence; sections without source evidence are explicitly marked empty rather than padded. Do NOT penalize for length differences between sections when they reflect real source coverage differences. |

items.name: Grounding Accuracy
items.description: How faithfully the wiki preserves source-native legal meaning, citation style, regulator identity, and section assignment without jurisdiction drift or invented institutions.
items.anchors:
| Score band | Anchor |
|---|---|
| 0-6 | Contains invented authorities, wrong jurisdiction markers, or claims not supportable by cited text. |
| 7-12 | Mostly grounded but includes notable jurisdiction drift, over-generalization, or source/style normalization errors. |
| 13-18 | Generally faithful, with some wording drift or ambiguous section placement but no major invented obligations. |
| 19-24 | Strongly grounded, preserves source-native legal style and institutions, with only minor compression issues. |
| 25-30 | Fully grounded, jurisdiction-consistent, and source-faithful with no invented authorities or citation-style drift. |

items.name: Actionability
items.description: Whether a new operator can immediately identify when to submit, what to submit, how to submit, and trigger events from the wiki with inline legal grounding.
items.anchors:
| Score band | Anchor |
|---|---|
| 0-3 | Mostly abstract text; when/what/how/trigger guidance is unclear or missing. |
| 4-6 | Some when/what/how/trigger elements are present, but users still cannot execute submissions reliably. |
| 7-9 | Core when/what/how/trigger guidance exists, with partial inline legal grounding and some ambiguity. |
| 10-12 | Clear when/what/how/trigger guidance with inline legal basis on most actionable claims. |
| 13-15 | End-to-end actionable guidance for when/what/how/trigger flows, with precise inline legal grounding and clear branching. |

items.name: Restraint
items.description: Degree to which content avoids unsupported inference and stays within source-backed regulatory facts.
items.anchors:
| Score band | Anchor |
|---|---|
| 0-1 | Multiple unsupported additions or speculative practical guidance. |
| 2 | One to two unsupported additions that could mislead implementation. |
| 3 | Mostly source-bounded, but includes minor overreach or redundant inferred detail. |
| 4 | Source-bounded with only negligible overreach and limited redundancy. |
| 5 | Strictly source-grounded, no speculative additions, minimal redundancy. |

items.name: Markdown Formatting
items.description: Conformance to the canonical heading tree, bullet structure, empty-section text, Obsidian footnote format, indentation rules, and prohibited-block constraints.

loader_interface.signature: SpecLoader.load(domain: str, layer: str, spec_type: str) -> dict | None

prompt.language: English instructions; Korean structure heading labels allowed

extract.prompt:
You are an expert tobacco regulation review analyst. Read the provided legal-review or secondary review documents and extract only source-grounded interpretive claims that clarify, narrow, validate, or nuance the Layer 1 legal requirements. Preserve jurisdiction exactly as written in the source. Do not invent authorities, institutions, or obligations. Prioritize review materials that directly analyze enforceability, scope, ambiguity, evidentiary expectations, product-category interpretation, regulator practice described in a credible review source, and conflicts between legal text and applied interpretation. Section mapping must target the existing canonical wiki tree only. If a review explains an existing Layer 1 rule, map it to the same section rather than creating a new section. Exclusions: no operational anecdotes, no unsupported practice myths, no country-external comparisons unless the source explicitly uses them to interpret the target jurisdiction. Every claim must be quote-grounded and include legal basis or review-source basis.
- Write footnote references using simple numeric ids such as [^1], [^2], [^3].
- Treat Layer 2 as legal review adjudication and clarification of Layer 1 content. Prioritize review findings that confirm, narrow, qualify, contradict, or materially detail an existing Layer 1 rule.
- If the review gives more concrete scope, trigger, exception, or filing detail for an otherwise vague Layer 1 rule, extract that clarification so it can be added under the same section.
- If the review appears to diverge from the statutory text, extract the divergence as a review conclusion rather than replacing the statute.
- If a legally material point is missing from Layer 1 but is clearly present in the legal-review source, extract it as review-backed supplementation for the same canonical section rather than treating it as a separate free-floating rule.

extraction_rules.task: Extract credible review-backed interpretive claims that strengthen the legal wiki without inventing obligations.
focus: review-backed interpretation of ambiguous legal text
review-identified evidentiary expectations or regulator reading
conflicts, uncertainty, or credibility caveats explicitly documented in the review source
extraction_rules.mapping_rule: Map review interpretation into the same canonical section as the underlying legal obligation.
exclusions: No non-target-jurisdiction examples unless source-explicit and interpretively necessary

update.prompt:
You are an expert regulatory wiki update author working on Layer 2 (law review integration). The existing wiki is provided as a JSON node array. Respond ONLY with a JSON array of MdDiff operations. Never return full markdown. Preserve the exact canonical heading tree and update the existing wiki in place using only the supplied extraction data. Use Layer 2 review-backed clarifications to tighten scope, ambiguity, applicability, and credibility under the existing legal sections. Do not overwrite direct statutory facts unless the review source directly justifies a correction. Allowed actions are update, insert_after, append_child, and delete. Every new factual node must remain source-cited. Do not create new headings, pseudo-headings, or unsourced practice claims. If a review only confirms an existing rule, minimally revise the existing node instead of duplicating it.

Respond ONLY with a JSON array of MdDiff objects using this exact schema: [{"action":"update","id":3,"content":"..."},{"action":"insert_after","id":7,"type":"list","parent":5,"indent":1,"content":"..."},{"action":"append_child","parent":4,"type":"list","indent":0,"content":"..."},{"action":"delete","id":10}]. Never use keys like op, path, node, replace, or add. Use only action/id/parent/type/indent/content/ref.
- Never update or delete an existing canonical heading node into a bullet, paragraph, or renamed heading. Canonical heading nodes must remain heading nodes with the exact same title.
- Never delete or rewrite an existing footnote definition node unless the corresponding cited statement is also removed. Preserve existing citation numbering and definitions whenever possible.
- If new review evidence qualifies or narrows an existing legal statement, prefer appending a clearly labeled clarification bullet after the original statement rather than replacing the original statement.
- When old and new evidence seem to conflict, keep the original legal fact and add a source-cited clarification explaining the review-backed interpretation or limitation.
- When you need to add interpretive or practical content for 분석결과제출, append bullets under the existing `## 분석결과제출` subtree instead of replacing that subtree with a custom label.
- When editing an existing paragraph or bullet that already contains footnote citations, preserve those existing citation references unless the cited statement itself is removed.
- If you add new facts to an existing cited paragraph, keep the original footnotes and add new citations rather than replacing the old citation block.
- Never drop existing footnote definition coverage through an update; preserve prior citations unless the underlying statement is explicitly deleted.
- Write footnote references using simple numeric ids such as [^1], [^2], [^3].
- **Every new content node MUST use `type: "list"` (bullet)**, not `type: "paragraph"`. When using `insert_after` or `append_child`, set `type: "list"` unless you are adding a heading node. Plain paragraph content nodes are disallowed for new substantive content.
- **Never insert a bullet that repeats an assertion already present in the section.** Before adding a clarification, scan the existing nodes at that parent and confirm the fact is not already stated. If the new bullet just rephrases an existing bullet from the same source, skip it.
- Write each heading and subheading as a self-contained section. Do not summarize a section as `same as above`, `동일`, `동일함`, or `신규제출과 동일`. If a heading is applicable, restate the actual content for that heading in full.
- Layer 2 should usually preserve the Layer 1 legal statement and add a review-backed clarification immediately after it.
- If the review materially narrows or qualifies the legal rule, phrase the new content as a legal-review finding such as `법률검토 자료에서는 ...로 검토되었다`, `법률검토 결과 ...로 파악되었다`, or `법률검토에서는 ...라는 해석이 제시되었다`.
- If the review gives more detailed filing scope, trigger, product coverage, evidentiary expectation, or authority interpretation than Layer 1, add that detail under the same heading instead of replacing the original legal rule.
- If the review appears inconsistent with the legal text, do not delete the legal text; preserve it and append a review-backed note explaining the difference.
- If the review introduces a legally material detail that Layer 1 omitted, add that detail under the same section as review-backed supplementation instead of replacing the original legal text.
- Footnote number sequencing: continue numbering from the highest existing footnote id in the current wiki. Never reuse a number that is already defined. If the existing wiki uses [^1] through [^12], begin new footnote definitions at [^13].
- Preserve Layer 1 legal content by default. Do not replace or compress an existing Layer 1 section unless the review source proves the Layer 1 wording is materially wrong.
- If the review adds nuance, scope, exceptions, or filing detail, append it next to the existing Layer 1 content instead of rewriting the original block.
- Preserve existing legal-source footnote references and their definitions whenever the underlying Layer 1 legal statement remains in the document.
- If an existing paragraph contains both legal fact and citations, do not rewrite that paragraph unless absolutely necessary; prefer adding a new clarification bullet after it.

format_rules.allowed_change: clarify, qualify, validate, or correct with review-backed evidence

revise.prompt:
You are revising a Layer 2 regulation wiki from explicit evaluator feedback. The existing wiki is provided as JSON nodes, and you must respond ONLY with a JSON array of MdDiff operations. Never return full markdown. Preserve the canonical heading tree. Improve interpretive precision, remove duplication, and tighten credibility using only source-backed review materials. If feedback indicates ambiguity, rewrite the relevant node to distinguish confirmed legal requirements from review-backed interpretation. Do not invent practical cases, do not add new headings, and keep every new or changed factual node source-cited.

Respond ONLY with a JSON array of MdDiff objects using this exact schema: [{"action":"update","id":3,"content":"..."},{"action":"insert_after","id":7,"type":"list","parent":5,"indent":1,"content":"..."},{"action":"append_child","parent":4,"type":"list","indent":0,"content":"..."}]. Never use keys like op, path, node, replace, or add. Use only action/id/parent/type/indent/content/ref.
- Never update or delete an existing canonical heading node into a bullet, paragraph, or renamed heading. Canonical heading nodes must remain heading nodes with the exact same title.
- Preserve existing footnote definition nodes unless you are deleting the cited statement itself.
- Do not replace an existing legal statement with a review statement when both can coexist. Prefer appending a clarification bullet that begins with wording equivalent to 'review 문서상 운영 설명으로는' or 'review 기준으로는'.
- When evaluator feedback says there is conflict, resolve it by preserving the statutory fact and adding a source-cited limitation, scope note, or interpretive caveat.
- When you need to add interpretive or practical content for 분석결과제출, append bullets under the existing `## 분석결과제출` subtree instead of replacing that subtree with a custom label.
- When editing an existing paragraph or bullet that already contains footnote citations, preserve those existing citation references unless the cited statement itself is removed.
- If you add new facts to an existing cited paragraph, keep the original footnotes and add new citations rather than replacing the old citation block.
- Never drop existing footnote definition coverage through an update; preserve prior citations unless the underlying statement is explicitly deleted.
- Write footnote references using simple numeric ids such as [^1], [^2], [^3].
- Write each heading and subheading as a self-contained section. Do not summarize a section as `same as above`, `동일`, `동일함`, or `신규제출과 동일`. If a heading is applicable, restate the actual content for that heading in full.
- When revising Layer 2, prefer preserving the original Layer 1 legal sentence and rewriting the Layer 2 addition around it.
- If clarification is needed, make the review status explicit in wording such as `법률검토 자료에서는`, `법률검토 결과`, or `법률검토상`.
- If evaluator feedback points to conflict, resolve it by keeping the statutory rule and rewriting the review note as a qualification, limitation, or contrary interpretation rather than deleting the legal rule.
- If evaluator feedback shows that Layer 1 omitted a legally material point already supported by the review source, restore that missing point as a review-backed supplement under the same heading.
- Footnote number sequencing: continue numbering from the highest existing footnote id in the current wiki. Never reuse a number that is already defined. If the existing wiki uses [^1] through [^12], begin new footnote definitions at [^13].
- Keep the original Layer 1 legal paragraph or bullet whenever it is still valid. Revise around it rather than replacing it.
- If feedback asks for more detail, add a new review-backed clarification bullet under the same heading instead of rewriting the Layer 1 sentence.
- Preserve existing legal-source footnote references and definitions whenever the cited Layer 1 statement is retained.
- Treat loss of prior legal citations as a failure condition; avoid edits that would remove them.

items.name: Citation Ratio
items.description: Ratio of substantive content blocks (bullets and paragraphs, excluding headings, empty markers, footnote defs) that include at least one inline footnote citation.

items.name: Coverage
items.description: How well Layer 2 clarifies the existing Layer 1 obligations across the canonical structure.
items.anchors:
| Score band | Anchor |
|---|---|
| 0-5 | Little useful clarification beyond Layer 1. |
| 6-10 | Some clarification but many important review-backed nuances missing. |
| 11-15 | Good clarification of several obligations; depth reflects source availability. |
| 16-20 | Strong review-backed clarification across most important sections. |
| 21-25 | Comprehensive source-grounded clarification across the full structure. Sections without review-backed evidence are acceptably empty — do NOT penalize for uneven section length when it reflects real source coverage. |

items.name: Grounding Accuracy
items.description: How faithfully the wiki distinguishes statutory fact from review-backed interpretation without jurisdiction drift or invention.
items.anchors:
| Score band | Anchor |
|---|---|
| 0-6 | Includes invented interpretations or jurisdiction drift. |
| 7-12 | Some real grounding, but interpretive claims overreach sources. |
| 13-18 | Mostly grounded, with a few ambiguous or over-broad restatements. |
| 19-24 | Strongly grounded and clear about interpretive status. |
| 25-30 | Fully source-faithful, jurisdiction-consistent, and interpretively precise. |

items.name: Actionability
items.description: How much Layer 2 helps a user understand how to read and trust the legal requirements in practice without inventing workflow stories.
items.anchors:
| Score band | Anchor |
|---|---|
| 0-3 | Adds little practical interpretive value. |
| 4-6 | Some useful clarifications, but still difficult to act on. |
| 7-9 | Helps users interpret obligations in several sections. |
| 10-12 | Strong interpretive usefulness with clear credibility cues. |
| 13-15 | Highly usable for understanding scope, ambiguity, and evidentiary expectations. |

items.name: Restraint
items.description: How well the wiki avoids unsupported practice claims or non-source-derived interpretation.
items.anchors:
| Score band | Anchor |
|---|---|
| 0-2 | Frequent unsupported interpretation. |
| 3-4 | Several overstated review claims. |
| 5-6 | Mostly restrained with minor overreach. |
| 7-8 | Strong restraint with rare overstatement. |
| 9-10 | Strictly source-bounded and disciplined. |

items.name: Markdown Formatting

extract.prompt:
You are an expert tobacco compliance case analyst. Read the provided real-world compliance, submission, enforcement, or workflow materials and extract only source-grounded practical claims that make the regulation wiki more operationally trustworthy. Preserve jurisdiction exactly as written. Extract concrete operational realities such as recurring failure points, authority feedback patterns, document package pitfalls, timing friction, approval bottlenecks, remediation expectations, and evidence packages actually requested in practice. Map every claim into the existing canonical heading tree under the legal obligation it helps operationalize. Exclusions: no invented anecdotes, no generic best practices, no fabricated examples, no foreign analogies unless the source explicitly ties them to the target jurisdiction. Every claim must be grounded in quote plus source/case basis. Narrow-case rule: if a case source documents only one product, one issue, or one internal exchange, keep the extracted claim equally narrow. Do not generalize the case into broad workflow guidance for all products or all filings.
- Do not surface concrete product names, internal product codes, or one-off case identifiers in extracted claims unless the legal source itself requires that exact identifier.
- Rewrite case evidence into anonymized operational tendencies such as `일부 신규제출 사례에서는`, `일부 변경제출 사례에서는`, or `정기제출 자료 검토에서는` when the exact product identity is not legally material.
- Preserve the operational lesson, not the product label.
- Write footnote references using simple numeric ids such as [^1], [^2], [^3].

extraction_rules.task: Extract operationally relevant, source-grounded practical claims that strengthen the wiki without inventing practice.
focus: case-backed remediation or recurring error patterns
extraction_rules.mapping_rule: Attach each practical claim to the canonical section for the underlying legal obligation it operationalizes.

update.prompt:
You are an expert regulatory wiki update author working on Layer 3 (practical compliance enrichment). The existing wiki is provided as a JSON node array. Respond ONLY with a JSON array of MdDiff operations. Never return full markdown. Preserve the exact canonical heading tree and prior legal layers. Add only source-backed practical details under the legal sections they operationalize: submission bottlenecks, package expectations, authority feedback, recurring mistakes, remediation patterns, and execution caveats. Do not invent stories, anecdotes, or regulator behavior. Do not create pseudo-headings like 실무:. Keep practical bullets concise, integrated, and source-cited.

Respond ONLY with a JSON array of MdDiff objects using this exact schema: [{"action":"update","id":3,"content":"..."},{"action":"insert_after","id":7,"type":"list","parent":5,"indent":1,"content":"..."},{"action":"append_child","parent":4,"type":"list","indent":0,"content":"..."},{"action":"delete","id":10}]. Never use keys like op, path, node, replace, or add. Use only action/id/parent/type/indent/content/ref.
- Never update or delete an existing canonical heading node into a bullet, paragraph, or renamed heading. Canonical heading nodes must remain heading nodes with the exact same title.
- Preserve existing footnote definition nodes unless the cited statement itself is removed.
- Layer 3 should enrich, not replace. Prefer append_child or insert_after over broad update when adding case/practical detail.
- If practical evidence seems to conflict with existing legal or review text, keep the earlier content and append a source-cited case note explaining the practical limitation or observed divergence.
- When you need to add interpretive or practical content for 분석결과제출, append bullets under the existing `## 분석결과제출` subtree instead of replacing that subtree with a custom label. Do not write advisory language such as 실무상 ~하면 된다, 준비사항, 직접적이다, 점검하면 쉽다, or generic recommendations unless the case source explicitly states that recommendation. Prefer source-bounded observational wording like `사례 문서에서는 ...가 확인되었다` or `해당 사례에서는 ...가 요청되었다`.
- When editing an existing paragraph or bullet that already contains footnote citations, preserve those existing citation references unless the cited statement itself is removed.
- If you add new facts to an existing cited paragraph, keep the original footnotes and add new citations rather than replacing the old citation block.
- Never drop existing footnote definition coverage through an update; preserve prior citations unless the underlying statement is explicitly deleted.
- Do not expose concrete product names, internal product codes, or one-off case titles in the final wiki unless legally necessary. Convert them into anonymized trend statements tied to submission stage or document type.
- Practical notes should read like observed tendencies (e.g. `일부 신규제출 자료에서는 ... 경향이 확인되었다`) rather than named case retellings.
- Write footnote references using simple numeric ids such as [^1], [^2], [^3].
- **Every new content node MUST use `type: "list"` (bullet)**, not `type: "paragraph"`. When using `insert_after` or `append_child`, set `type: "list"` unless you are adding a heading node. Plain paragraph content nodes are disallowed for new substantive content.
- **Never insert a bullet that repeats an assertion already present in the section.** Before adding a clarification, scan the existing nodes at that parent and confirm the fact is not already stated. If the new bullet just rephrases an existing bullet from the same source, skip it.
- Write each heading and subheading as a self-contained section. Do not summarize a section as `same as above`, `동일`, `동일함`, or `신규제출과 동일`. If a heading is applicable, restate the actual content for that heading in full.
- Footnote number sequencing: continue numbering from the highest existing footnote id in the current wiki. Never reuse a number that is already defined. If the existing wiki uses [^1] through [^12], begin new footnote definitions at [^13].
- Preserve all earlier-layer legal and review references by default. Layer 3 should add practical interpretation around them, not replace them.
- If a practical note is added under a heading, append it after the existing cited content instead of rewriting the earlier block.
- Do not collapse multiple earlier citations into a single new practical citation when the earlier legal or review statements still remain relevant.
- Keep prior references visible whenever their content is still present in the section.

format_rules.allowed_change: operational enrichment under existing legal headings

revise.prompt:
You are revising a Layer 3 regulation wiki from explicit evaluator feedback. The existing wiki is provided as JSON nodes, and you must respond ONLY with a JSON array of MdDiff operations. Never return full markdown. Preserve the canonical heading tree and prior legal layers. Improve operational clarity, reduce duplication, and keep practical content tightly tied to actual source-backed cases or workflow materials. Never invent anecdotes, regulator behavior, or implied expectations. Keep all practical changes concise and directly usable.

Respond ONLY with a JSON array of MdDiff objects using this exact schema: [{"action":"update","id":3,"content":"..."},{"action":"insert_after","id":7,"type":"list","parent":5,"indent":1,"content":"..."},{"action":"append_child","parent":4,"type":"list","indent":0,"content":"..."}]. Never use keys like op, path, node, replace, or add. Use only action/id/parent/type/indent/content/ref.
- Never update or delete an existing canonical heading node into a bullet, paragraph, or renamed heading. Canonical heading nodes must remain heading nodes with the exact same title.
- Preserve existing footnote definition nodes unless the cited statement itself is removed.
- Treat Layer 3 as additive. If feedback says existing legal/review content is incomplete, append a case-grounded note before considering replacement.
- When practical evidence is narrow, keep it narrow and append a caveat rather than rewriting broader prior content.
- When you need to add interpretive or practical content for 분석결과제출, append bullets under the existing `## 분석결과제출` subtree instead of replacing that subtree with a custom label. Remove generic advice, inferred best practices, and broad checklist language unless the case source explicitly supports them. Keep Layer 3 additions as case observations, case-requested materials, case-encountered issues, or case-confirmed practical facts.
- When editing an existing paragraph or bullet that already contains footnote citations, preserve those existing citation references unless the cited statement itself is removed.
- If you add new facts to an existing cited paragraph, keep the original footnotes and add new citations rather than replacing the old citation block.
- Never drop existing footnote definition coverage through an update; preserve prior citations unless the underlying statement is explicitly deleted.
- Remove explicit product names, product codes, and one-off case titles unless they are legally indispensable.
- Rewrite practical examples into anonymized trend statements by submission stage, period, or document class while preserving the operational meaning.
- Write footnote references using simple numeric ids such as [^1], [^2], [^3].
- Write each heading and subheading as a self-contained section. Do not summarize a section as `same as above`, `동일`, `동일함`, or `신규제출과 동일`. If a heading is applicable, restate the actual content for that heading in full.
- Footnote number sequencing: continue numbering from the highest existing footnote id in the current wiki. Never reuse a number that is already defined. If the existing wiki uses [^1] through [^12], begin new footnote definitions at [^13].
- Preserve existing legal and review references unless the corresponding statement is explicitly removed.
- When improving clarity, rewrite the practical addition first and leave earlier cited legal/review statements intact whenever possible.
- Do not let a practical rewrite erase previously cited legal or review content. Add a new practical note rather than replacing the old cited block.
- If an edit would remove prior reference coverage, prefer a smaller additive edit.

items.name: Citation Ratio
items.description: Ratio of substantive content blocks (bullets and paragraphs, excluding headings, empty markers, footnote defs) that include at least one inline footnote citation.

items.name: Coverage
items.description: How well Layer 3 enriches the existing structure with practical compliance insight where source-backed cases exist.
items.anchors:
| Score band | Anchor |
|---|---|
| 0-4 | Minimal or irrelevant practical enrichment. |
| 5-8 | Some practical content but sparse. |
| 9-12 | Useful enrichment in several sections where sources support it. |
| 13-16 | Strong practical enrichment across key sections. |
| 17-20 | Comprehensive source-grounded enrichment. Sections without practical evidence are acceptably empty — do NOT penalize for uneven section length when it reflects real source coverage. |

items.name: Grounding Accuracy
items.description: How faithfully the practical content stays tied to actual case/workflow evidence without invention or jurisdiction drift.
items.anchors:
| Score band | Anchor |
|---|---|
| 0-6 | Invented practical content, generalized advice unsupported by case evidence, or wrong-jurisdiction practice. |
| 7-12 | Significant unsupported operational advice or extrapolation beyond the case. |
| 13-18 | Mostly grounded, but includes some broad recommendations not directly evidenced. |
| 19-24 | Strongly grounded and clearly case-backed, with only minor extrapolation. |
| 25-30 | Fully case-grounded, narrow where the evidence is narrow, and free from unsupported operational advice. |

items.name: Actionability
items.description: How much the wiki helps a real user execute, anticipate, and troubleshoot compliance work using source-backed practical knowledge.
items.anchors:
| Score band | Anchor |
|---|---|
| 0-4 | Little operational value. |
| 5-8 | Some useful guidance but hard to execute from. |
| 9-12 | Clear practical value for several workflows. |
| 13-16 | Strong operational usefulness with concrete troubleshooting value. |
| 17-20 | Highly usable as a working compliance reference. |

items.name: Restraint
items.description: How well the wiki avoids over-generalizing from limited cases or inventing practical norms.
items.anchors:
| Score band | Anchor |
|---|---|
| 0-2 | Frequent invention or over-generalization. |
| 3-4 | Several unsupported practice claims. |
| 5-6 | Mostly restrained with occasional overreach. |
| 7-8 | Strong restraint with minor issues. |
| 9-10 | Strictly case-grounded and careful. |

items.name: Markdown Formatting
